play_store_reviews.text: Intuitive & simple! I am SO happy with this app/web system. I'm typically a paper to-do list snob, but I've had so much going on with work that I needed something electronic. Asana is easy to use, incredibly intuitive, and I know this sounds silly, but the way they "celebrate" checking things off the list makes this the first electronic to-do list I've ever used that gives me the same satisfaction as physically crossing things off a paper list!

The service is pretty good, although I preferred the web version. When my year subscription came up, I no longer had a use case and went to cancel. Asana offered a reduced-feature subscription and it looked like it would be free, so I kept it. I was surprised to find a charge for $264. When I reached out for a refund, they told me to kick rocks. Deceptive practices and terrible customer service.

I used to love this app, but it keeps getting more and more squirrely. It won't maintain sorting by due date in tasks. When I move an item in a subtask list, it moves the item back to the original location. The widget has recently become buggy as well, sorting in no particular order. I am currently looking for a replacement. I don't necessarily need all the bells and whistles. But I do need something that can at least perform reasonable basic functions for sorting and grouping.

Update... everything has gotten substantially better over the past few years, but recently took a small step backwards. App is now laggy and can't catch up. Hopefully it'll get resolved soon. App is still lacking... - When typing in a field for editing a task or sub-task, the phones keyboard covers the field. If you try to scroll down, it deselects the field.

As a general rule, I love Asana. I'm a long time user and I regularly use the web version on my laptop. However, today I tried to open the mobile version and it told me that I needed to update the app in order to use it ... but the update is no longer compatible with my phone. That's incredibly disappointing. if your app has gotten so complicated that it won't work on anything except the newest phone, it's probably time to roll back on some of those complex operations, at least on mobile. 😖

It's an amazing organization product. I use for personal as well as work. I wish there were some more affordable options for singles and small groups that use it, to obtain some key features like start and end date/time. The only improvements I could suggest is more specifically tailored templates for industries, i.e Agricultural, event planning or Retail purposes.

I love to use Asana. It is a lifeline for our very small business. However, this mobile app can be far more disappointing and frustrating than useful. It is especially frustrating because you cannot use the web app on a mobile device, but using mobile app almost always creates more work. Biggest downfalls are 1. You cannot duplicate a task which is basically the only way we use the program. 2. You will be editing a description & mid typing it will completely reformat the entire description.

it's ok. the desktop app is better. it's nice that the two synchronize with each other essentially instantaneously. the phone app defaults all your new tasks to this annoying 'do now' category and you can't change it or change the default. so if you want to create 5 new tasks quickly on your phone and sort them into the proper category you have to do it all one at a time. the collaboration options are similarly limited which actually makes the paid version *less useful* than the free version.

Honestly, I'd been looking for an app like this for a while. I could find a planner type app to keep track of my homework, and Google tasks/sheets/etc. just wasn't cutting it. Now I can really quickly add and check off tasks from my phone home screen, and it's easy to remember what to do. Also, a narwhal flies across the screen when you complete a task, and it gives me the dopamine I need to get through the day.

It seemed ok, a little learning curve. It's definitely what I call groupware, so you have an extra layer which adds complexity when using as a personal tool. It also required me to log into app vida desktop to use features, more complexity. I was an IT manager for many years and know the company that purchased them, we used them. I searched for another app and I'm quite happy. I don't mind paying, want simplicity.

This is a very capable application but only for those who can afford it. I understand there needs to be some kind of business model but it's a shame there isn't a full version for education accounts; wait until you "complete a task" and try to view what was completed — hook and sinker, pay up! — I happily returned to the competitor.

Don't try to get support. I initially submitted a ticket 6 months ago and took 2 months for the first reply, my issue was never resolved and i learned they lied to me. Opened another ticket for a different issue, got a "how was our support" survey when I didnt even get a reply. Tried following up 2-3 times on each. They keep blaming it on a new ticketing system but they get some emails and not all? It's a joke. I've cancelled our premium teams subscription and moving to a competitor.

Recently this app has started crashing, adds random spacing between lines in descriptions and doesn't allow edits, and no longer shows all tasks. I've also been frustrated for a while that you can't use tags in a personal account, and that they removed keyboard shortcuts and the ability to total hours. The latest changes are the push I needed to switch to another system.

I used to love asana, but now I can't even get logged in to see my projects. I only use asana on my phone and tablet. Every time I try to login, it sends me into my browser app. After I login in the browser, there is a button that says "Continue To App", but the button won't do anything. Tried on both devices and even after reinstalling, no luck. Unfortunate.

doesn't provide the same task grouping functionality as the website, and the web interface is a pita to use on a phone - in most situations where I'm trying to use Asana on my mobile, the website is better to use, and I'm having to jump through several hoops to stop it saying 'use the app' at every turn.

They conveniently omit from their pricing/plan comparison page that you must sign up for a minimum of 2 users, so it's useless for self-employed consultants or anyone who wants to organize a variety of projects on their own without the need to bring collaborators into the tedium of task management as users. They name the tool a hip-sounding yoga word (which they mispronounce) and think they're reinventing the wheel. They'll likely fizzle out amid competition or get bought out.
I like using Asana because it makes teamwork easier. I can assign tasks and set deadlines quickly. However, it can be a bit confusing for beginners at first. Overall, it is very useful.

Awful. it was great and looked promising until I couldn't delete a useless workspace that kept coming up first in the app and wouldn't switch permanently to the other one, which made the widget useless. on trying to delete that workspace I couldn't because I was the billing user even though I'm only on a free trial. tried support, which directed me to Emalie in sales, which directed me back to support and to a page that literally had no interactive options. Thanks for wasting so much time.

I've been using this product for over 10 years. I logged in yesterday and my entire account is gone. I've now received a response to my customer service inquiry that the Personal Projects product was discontinued. and rather than do the right thing and transfer this data into another one of their products or retain it in an archive for a reasonable period of time so that it could be recovered, Asana permanently deleted everything. I'm absolutely done with Asana.

unsubscribing from the torrent if spam emails is near enough impossible. the app is ok, it doesn't do what I need it to, but I can see some value in it. but leaving after the trial without hundreds of emails a month is far from satisfactory

I prefer the functionality of the desktop version but the app I serviceable when I'm running between stores at work. However, I'm now unable to tag anyone which is extremely frustrating when trying to communicate with my various teams.
The absolute best task management app available. I've tried them all. A real game changer and a generous free plan.
Asana is really helpful for planning work and managing tasks. Everything is well organized and easy to follow. It helps me stay productive and focused. Overall, it’s a great app for work and teamwork
The headings in a landscape mode take 2/3 of the screen so I can only see 1 and a half of a task at a time. So quite useless app...
Great app for task management and team collaboration. Easy to organize projects and track progress, but advanced features require a paid plan.
More glitches than it should have, and tiny subject fields to read. might be nice on a big screen, but a Trimble TCS5 leaves less than and inch of usable display within all of your buttons on screen

In the latest version, in Board view, the filter/view/new task control covers over the last viewable item on the screen if the list makes it to the bottom of the page or further. Thus, to interact with the last task, you have to flip to List view. This is a major inconvenience. App also randomly resets to home if I switch to another app for too long and come back. This is just another time waster. It's like nobody has ever seen a functional UI before.
Asana is excellent for managing projects and workflows. Task tracking and team collaboration are seamless. The interface is clean and professional. Ideal for business and team productivity.
When choosing a person's name (some of them) when tagging them, it just won't be clicked. It used to work fine.

After newest update doesn't recognize all the fields in "my tasks" to reorganize tasks. before update that would happen sometimes and a refresh would fix it. After update it's not working at all. I'm not working for free as a IV&V test engineer to troubleshoot it with the SW engineers. I did that for a living and it's insulting to the tradecraft to expect customers to do it for free.

This app has consistent widget issues. Seems like every other update breaks the widget so you either have to refresh or log back into the app for it to show your tasks. I really like this platform, and I love using it both on my phone and browser because of the instant sync, but the widget feature has got to be one of the most frustrating and annoying because I would like to be able to rely on it but just can't.
I don't know what you guys did in the latest update but I'm unable to tag people in comments/tasks on the mobile app. very frustrating.
simple, easy to use, but robust enough for varying project complexity. keeps me focused and not overwhelmed.

I usually like this app, but the latest update contains a bug that is making it unusable. I can't shift tasks between custom sections. For example a new task that is created under the default "to schedule" section can't be moved to "this week", when I click to open the drop-down menu it tells me I'm offline but that is incorrect, I'm indeed online

Feedback: the widget needs a refresh for when the phone starts up w/o internet. it says it's blocked by my admin, but no. I was just in airplane mode. tapped everywhere, no way to try again once my internet connected. original review: 1 it's too easy to complete tasks while scrolling, 2 it doesn't show my projects exactly like it does on the web. Still, it's super convenient to be able to add to my tasks while in a meeting, rather than waiting until I get to my desk.

The app doesn't update task statuses. By default it should filter out tasks that have already been completed and one day it just stopped working so I can't check at a glance what I have due each day. I've run into other issues too. This app is pointless if it doesn't work as intended.
I CAN'T MENTION COLLABORATORS with @ when leaving a comment, nothing happens when I click on suggested names!!! Impossible to work because of this.
Please fix bugs for android tablet, texts are hid too low on the lowest screen so we cannot read it as we type
I am unable to mention the person on ticket, might be there is bug, please fix it, I have updated the app recently.
bad experience, app is slower than web version, important comments don't even loads on time

App is a complete mess since the last update. The My Tasks "Today" section shows completed tasks and tasks for a different day. Some sections don't show when trying to move tasks. They've somehow made the "swipe to complete" functionality more sensitive so I'm constantly completing tasks accidentally when trying to scroll. Completely unusable currently.

Asana has completely transformed the way I manage my tasks and projects. The interface is clean, intuitive, and easy to navigate, making it simple to organize work whether I’m managing personal tasks or collaborating with a team. I love the flexibility of creating projects, assigning tasks, setting deadlines,and tracking progress all in one place. The integrations with other apps like Slack, Google Drive, and Microsoft Teams make workflows seamless.

Asana is fantastic for managing more detailed workflows. While we loved Trello for simple lists, Asana allows us to see timelines and dependencies, which is crucial for our team projects. It’s very reliable on Fiji’s networks and the mobile app is great for checking tasks while on the bus or away from the desk.
Almost never works correctly on Android as it shows tasks from weeks ago that have been completed (not visible on laptop, but the app doesn't update properly) version 8.75.7
Every other day, I have to uninstall and reinstall this app because it won't let me reorder tasks or add/delete new ones. Clearing the cache doesn't help. Such a hassle!

I was testing. it says it has a free version and a trial period to see some pay options. after the trial ends it just lock my asana webpage and block my access to my work unless I paid and upgrade my account. I lost my work here. if you have a free version, just remove the paid feature. but block the entire work?

Excellent app. I use the free one, but I would be willing to pay for an individual license if I could get access to more features. £5 a month or £50 a year would be a sweet point. Asana are missing a potential revenue stream. Not all professionals are in teams and require the bells and whistles. But overall an excellent application and one that I use on a a daily basis to keep on track with all my projects.

App is poor in comparison to website. I can't see a way to see *all* tasks and not just mine and I keep having to set the sort order of the tasks each time I load into the app. I'd also like the full calendar view from the website and ability to select multiple tasks on the task list and calendar to change items like dates in bulk. Generally I wish I could assign more than one user to a task too.

For being a project management app it makes managing projects particularly difficult. Why on earth adding or removing a workspace or just renaming it is so tricky that I've been searching for a solution for the last 15 minutes and still haven't found one?? Fire your UI team!

Later edit: customer support has been fast and incredibly helpful to fully restore my lost account and everything is in order. It was a good scare but CS saved the day. Infuriating and confusing, the app is not safe to use. After using it during a year for about 5-6 projects, I got logged out from my account yesterday and couldn't log back in, it said my email doesn't have an account at all. As if my 1 year work never existed. ALL my work is gone with no clue on how to recover it.

Good, but lots of room for improvement. The "My Tasks" page really needs work. Specifically I would like to be able to change what swiping the tasks does, which for me would be nothing as I am constantly accidentally swiping and marking them complete. And secondly I would like to be able to change which Priority is shown/used for sorting, as the priority marked in the Project is relevant to me and I do not mark tasks with my own priority levels.

Amazing app, terrible UI. I use Asana for everything, but the inability to create tags, create sections within subtasks and the apparent glitch when trying to add anymore than 3 subtasks (where the screen dims and it won't type, no matter how many times you refresh, close the program etc -- on the mobile app, make it almost impossible to use this app on the go. I am a busy, work from home, mother of 2. I do not always have time to pull out a laptop and I need my phone to do most of my work.
Asana is 📊💪! 😄 Keeps me on track, tasks sorted, and team's all aligned! 👍

My work uses Asana as a calendar. The app has a problem: when I open the calendar, it shows the current month and year, but tasks are from exactly one year ago. When I try to scroll to the next month, it fixes the calendar date, but then I have to scroll one full year forward to reach today. What the hell? also there are many problems, for example I still can't find a way to make a task public...
Many errors , not updating on my phone. is taking too long to tag others
Best company on this planet. They care. They update. They developing. All they're doing have head and heel. Simply perfect app. Congrats. I love asana❤️!
It's a bit tricky to use. It's not easy to add a member.

Post the most recent Pixel update, the "create" button when adding a task is now under the Pixel buttons (notably the accessibility button which is hilarious) rendering the app unusable on mobile, since adding tasks on the go is, IMHO, the most important use case for mobile.
Major bug issues in latest update. no matter on data or wifi, attempting to change section of a task results in an error " you are offline try again later".
Following the latest update, I am unable to update task section on app. It says "You're offline. Try again later" but I can make other edits. Quite frustrating!
Push ads in the main notification channel telling me to try the desktop app. Designate a separate channel for ads so users don't need to disable all notifications to hide ads.
logged off, cannot log back in. lacking critical abilities. for example, no way to add tasks I assigned to home.

Works well and has a good level of customisation even for free users. However has a huge drawback: needs to be online at all times. As someone who commutes to work it means it's not accessible on my daily commute. Nor it's accessible on flights I take for work trips.. It's supposed to aid in productivity and management, but such limitations on when it's available makes it quite frustrating. I'll keep using it as my supervisor chose it for our team, but wouldn't choose it myself.

Was great until recent. Trying to add to the discription box via my phone and the cursor jumps around and then the description box randomly adds huge amounts of space to what typing. So everything ends up all over the page. Also would be great if you could add the ability to duplicate a task from within the app. Will come back and update my review when things improve but all round it is a fantastic app if using the computer version, just needs some work for mobile compatibility.

Simply beautiful. The only thing I would change, I'd add the ability to set not just the date of the reminders but also the time. And when setting the time, I'd add the ability to use previously selected times. Example: if last week I set a task as due at 3 PM, then when I set next task, I'd like the 3 PM option (and all the 5 last used hours) to be displayed as quick options.

Frustrating that I cannot move tasks on the list to other sections or reorder them reliably using the mobile app. sometimes it works, sometimes it doesn't. often I need to swipe down to refresh before any changes appear. It's frustratingly unreliable and I often end up having to use the website or wait until I'm home on the PC to put tasks in the correct section or order. Disappointing really because I do rely on Asana currently.

There's no option to get reminders. I don't want email notifications, but push notifications. It's not just this app, but trello and clickup don't have this feature as well. This is very simple but extremely important feature. Should've a reminders option like Google and outlook calendar. Reminders should've options - at the time, 1, 3, 6, 12 hours before the time set with due date or 1 day before due date.

Asana is overpriced and forces you to pay for at least two users, even if you're solo. Their customer service is rigid and unhelpful – I barely used the premium features, but they refused to issue any refund. Once they have your money, it's gone. The app offers little value for the cost, and better, cheaper alternatives exist. If you want flexibility and fair pricing, look elsewhere.

The app is actually quite good .... the customer service leaves a LOT to be desired!! They billed my card 4 times ($150 per time) when it was supposed to have been switched temporarily to a free acct. I was talked into this vs canceling. Well now it IS canceled! I will fight for my $600 back. They charged my card without authorization. Their service dept was useless. They caused me more hours of work and still did not refund....... BEWARE!!!!!

To say it falls short compared to the the desktop version is an understatement. It won't allow you to paste your clipboard as multiple subtasks. You can't manipulate subtasks to move them to another parent, nor do anything but complete, rename, or delete them. It offers a single very basic widget. And it won't get better even on the paid plans. One thing is to simplify and another is to decimate to the point of uselessness. The productivity of a team starts at the individual level.
Incredibly helpful in organizing projects. I love the seamless movement from pc to mobile devices.

I installed this app a long while ago, it automatically started a trial right away. I was unprepared to do a whole setup and missed the trial. Now I want to check the prices and features but when I click to learn about it, sends me an email with a link, that brings me right back to the app that yet agains brings me to the button in app to get the same email. It's a frustrating loop that I don't have time for, and I am not going to search for a pc that I don't own, for the extra effort. Lost my $

Asana is great. What I love the most is that they offer tons of features for free users, this is especially useful for my small business. They also update frequently in my experience. Not perfect by any means, but one of the best options out there. It would be great if they added options for marketing agencies like scheduling within Asana to post on multiple social accounts.

Asana has been my favourite tool for tasks management. This app was very useful when you want to manage your tasks on a mobile device. The more recent versions of the app are no longer compatible with my phone and the installed version does no longer allow me to use it.

Love Asana for its flexibility, which makes it great for easily managing my daily to-do lists and all sorts of personal projects. I'd give it five stars, but the widget recently stopped working, displaying an error message that reads: "Something went wrong with this widget. Please reconfigure or open the app to get the latest updates." I've tried both with no luck.

So they advertise a free platform, but really it's not. You can't even get a basic free version. You'll have to pay eventually. So me and my team of 5, a small self-funded start up, bite the bullet and get the Premium plan. Granted they won't charge us for 30 days, but we're **FORCED** to stay on the trial plan so after I spent AN ENTIRE DAY BUILDING 4 MASSIVE PROJECTS, the stupid platform LOCKS MY DATA in THE MOST BASIC FIELD.... the status field.... and tells me I have to upgrade.

I love Asana. It makes it a pleasure to have systems and take control of your time being organized. I just couldn't live without it now. Such a beautiful and intuitive platform. It makes planning and collaborations a complete joy. I wish the premium plans were also thought with individual users in mind, as I find it quite expensive for just myself.

Very misleading view of what a free version is. Private projects are not listed in the pricing comparison. If you have a private project after the trial, you have to dig through the "add a pricing plan" and find your way to make the project public. You do have access and can continue to use free but it is a pain to figure out and feels like extortion until you find the small print path to access the data that you just spent time and energy bringing a team (or family) up to speed on how to use and enter.

Their app is half baked compared to site. Create a deadline for something, and then make 5 subtasks with deadlines that all come earlier, leading up to the major deadline. On the website, if you go to tasks created by me, and then sort by due date, both the major tasks and sub-tasks will show - telling me what I have to do today. On the app, only the major tasks show - you have to click a major tasks to pull up the list of subtasks. Easy to miss a sub-deadline because of this.

Your app requires me to log in every time I minimize it, so, whenever I get links and invites to join a workspace, what follows is a log in requests even though I'd just logged in seconds before. After the log in, it doesn't take me to the invite, just to my homepage or tasks or something else. This is incredibly frustrating. One star.

I started using this at the end of last semester when I was feeling overwhelmed. Really helped me get organized and get a clear idea of how to stay on track and finish strong. I'm currently setting up for this coming semester and it's really easy to build out each class ahead of time so I can just copy and paste once the classes are open. I like how it has calendar integrations so I get reminders.

Overall a very good platform which has most of the features I was after, but the deal breaker for me was the inability to display recurring events individually on the calendar. A brief Google search shows that this has been a popular request for several years now, but for some reason, the developers have decided not to include this. Even the most basic calendar apps allow users to do this. I've sadly had to uninstall the app for this reason alone.

FOR THE MOBILE EXPERIENCE. 5 Stars for desktop experience though. I find quite confusing to get the same things done in my cellphone than in my pc. Its really frustating, can't get subtasks where I want to. Is either bad designed or is me, using the tool wrong. Either ways, is bad design anyways, cause it should be intuitive. Maybe I'll go back to wunderlist.

Great tool for an organization's first step into work management. Effective for managing personal tasks and task-oriented team projects. For five stars, add more fine-tuning of scheduling recurring tasks, allow for a "browse to and link" for local network documents instead of attaching them to the task, and add more "pop" to the (desktop) sidebar's Team headers (the menu items and headers are too similar). The team headers are much better in the mobile app.

The company hides the fact that there is no single seat available wasting tons of time for people that spent their trial setting everything up only to find out that it will cost you a min of $50 month. You have tons of feedback about this (having spent some time searching a little too late) why in the hell don't you enable single seats? Honestly the way you advertise "pay by the seat' is deceptive at best.

It is so disappointing, being a huge fan of Asana, to attempt to use this mobile app. It's pretty useless for most things, takes forever to refresh task status, and navigating projects is nearly impossible. it's decent for to-do type tasks, but come on, if you're using Asana, you're not using it as a grocery list. In the unlikely scenario that you are though, go for it, it's 5 stars then.

I like it guys. Love what you have done here, well done. Just one thing. Can you please consider an option of an less than premium subscription? For example for 2usd per person, to be able to enable up to 2 or 3 selectable premium features. Thousands of free users and teams like mine (10 people) would do the upgrade in a heartbeat. Other than that, keep the good job guys. Update: my tasks should be sorted by due date by default.

Utterly useless for getting things done. Instead of notifying you of individual tasks at their due time, it sends one notification a day. But that's only IF it puts out a notification at all, which it seldom does. Basically, you waste a bunch of time creating a work stream just to never know you have tasks due. The app is super slow as well.

Asana is GREAT. ABSOLUTELY FANTASTIC. It's also easy to get used to. my only major trouble has been easily adding new teammates. I find it's not very seamless in terms of allowing my team to easily get on a platform. they always experience problems when they have to log in/signup and it's just a real frustration to actually get them in and working on projects ASAP! Good app, but just one drawback for me.

I used to use Google Tasks for setting 'reminders', both online and offline. However, when I switched to Asana, I noticed that the reminder feature does not work properly. Despite giving notification access, I never receive push notifications for due times. This is a drawback compared to Google Tasks, as many other users have also reported the same issue. (No push notification in Asana app)

Really ready to use too. Highly recommend. Update: they have terrible customer service. I have been attempting for weeks to understand my subscription and the company refuses to call. I've gotten 10 emails from 10 different team members. No helpful responses. Just being referred to the FAQ website. Extremely disappointed.

I love this app! But I wish the "free" plan had a bit more to offer. And apart from that, there should be a feature where all our important events (such as open evenings, or sports tryouts, etc) are on a calendar. Now I'm quite new to this app and stilll on trial but I love this. It's just need a bit more improvement on the calendar.

The program is lovely but the pricing described is highly misleading -- count on paying at least twice what is quoted. The trick is in the "per seat" language - if there's only one of you, too bad, you must subscribe for at least 2 seats, i.e. twice the price that you were quoted. Bad start to a relationship because it suggests that you'll be hoodwinked multiple more times along the way.

The mobile app was clunky and slow to load on my Galaxy S9, often times giving me push notifications 30-60 minutes after I had already seen the update on the desktop site (I hate the desktop site, too). Now, in the last two weeks, the mobile app won't even open on my phone. It just says Asana on a white screen and stays there forever. It crashes my phone if I don't disable it. Useless app.

I've been using asana for a long long time (5 yrs?) but this new update drives me absolutely crazy. It's so difficult to clearly see what I have on my list to do for today, I need to move between different sections so it's easy to miss out on something - totally against what this app is supposed to do for you. Deeply disappointed, might consider moving to a competition after so many years :(

New bugs make the mobile app seem unreliable. I'd be happier with the 2015 version. (1) When re-ordering tasks on mobile, the item "jumps" back to it's original location before jumping back up. Cognitively, this visual movement makes no sense. Also, it makes my eyes and head hurt. Worse, more than 75% of the time, the item "jumps" back to the original location, "jumps" up to the dragged location, then suddenly appears back at the original location. (2) Trouble editing task.

There's a weird visual delay with Android 10, Pixel 2: upon opening the app, it flashes on-screen, then goes away as if it crashed. About 2-3 seconds later, it opens properly without further interaction. Or after selecting the app icon a second time in rapid succession. This would make it 5 stars. Other updates: adding better inbox filters would make the inbox usable...and dark mode would be nice.

The experience is so close to be good, but it has a big issue, you can not control the zoom. In my case, all the interface and the components in general are too big to provide a good interaction. If the app add a way to control the zoom, the overall experience will improve a lot.

Had the app for years. It just randomly logged me out and know it's think I have no account and when I put in my email to login it thinks it's a new email and wants me to sign up (even though I still get Asana emails, so it clearly is the right email). Now I've lost all my projects and hundreds of entries. It wants me to just start again. I can't trust this app now. It could just do it again. I have uninstalled. It was a good app otherwise. What a shame. Don't recommend.

Just brilliant. I've been using Asana for years now, it never disappoints. Clean overviews, useful sort functions, and a unicorn that pops up every once in a to-do. I've recommended it to many friends and co-workers, because I have been testing many free-to-use Task-Apps until now and always ended up being disappointed. Asana changed that, thanks for the great work!

Tried it once years ago, Never used it, no subscription, opted out of emails. Yet they are still emailing me from other accounts. So they still have my info. They also started sending these emails saying "we changed our policy, reactivate your account before your information is deleted" I was fine with it, but they kept emailing the samething over for a year, which made me think, you guys are lying to force me onto your app. I also cannot unsubcribe from new emails, no opt out button at bottem.

The app was good and really helpful for me and my team. However, it started charging us more in the last few months without prior notice. They only sent an email once and it went directly to spam. Without even waiting for the acknowledgement of the users they started charging more than the original amount.

App is good, but with game-breaking issues, like task comments in Inbox are sorted by date so you'll see oldest first, and you have to scroll down through 30+ half a year old comments to see new ones, without any options/settings to change that sorting or limit number of comments shown. If you want to use app to quickly see new updates/comments - prepare for endless scrolling and confusing UI. It's been like that for 2 years, and probably never gets fixed.

Good but I can give no more than three stars, the reasons are: 1. The refreshing is slow and no option for background refreshing, something I have to wait a bit when I open the screen, and I have to manually swipe down to make sure it's updated. 2. Lack of personality settings, for example, it's easily to "complete" a task by mistake when I swipe left on the screen, but I can't turn off such settings for this.

Get organized, get Asana. (Don't use that, 😑) Anyway, just take a few minutes to put your day's task and visualize it everywhere you need. Also can share the jobs(tasks) with teammates ( or wife) to monitor milestones or set due dates, get help from them... The app works really well, like paper, but the phone it's difficulter to lose.

The browser application and the mobile application work side-by-side together to make my work possible at any location. I like the third party cloud file linking (Onebox, GDrive, Dropbox, etc), so that my colleagues and I can keep files linked for access in one single area. One extra step for the development team would be to include the 'move task to other subtask' option available in the mobile application.

Asana is good, the app is also good I'm getting used to it still, but still good. My complaint at this point would be for the widget on Android. It shows what has been recently assigned. There is no way to change all your tasks in the widget by due today or due date, like the calendar in the app. It'd be nice to have it visible as a constant reminder of to dos rather than opening the app or logging in a a computer. Thank you.

We us asana on a daily basis to organize and assign tasks at my company. It has been such a great tool to use. It may seem complicated at first but trust me once you have the vision for the organization you want it is super easy and clean. The app has been amazing as well especially when away from my computer. I can assign tasks, set due dates and complete tasks all from my phone. It also has a cool widget for android users.

Has potential if app wasn't so glitchy. So many things sometimes work/sometimes don't. It's a bit frustrating. At the moment none of my due tasks are showing in the Inbox where they were previously. Boards often don't show at all until restart app. Also not enough flexibility on notification settings. Otherwise this would be fantastic. The premium version is too expensive for average non business user. Free version glitchy as mentioned.

New update making it impossible to type in description! Everytime I try to type in a new or existing task I get a few sentences in and then it just starts adding/entering to new lines so much so that I can't even scroll to the bottom to delete them or stop them. Every key I press seems to add more new lines. Someone needs to fix this asap bc the app is completely unusable this way!

I've always been a huge fan of Asana. However, the app used to lack a lot of the functionalities that were available on desktop so I stopped using it for a time. Now that the app is more complete, I'm back at it and couldn't be happier. Asana is a fantastic way to organize multiple projects and stay on track with all of them!

Useful on desktop. However, simple functions that are usable on desktop are not usable on mobile Examples of bugs: - does not allow you to move sections around - does not allow you to move task into other sections - portfolios and projects not updating/loading unless you log out and back in - cant view the messages on the message board Conclusion: Use desktop

Asana is great! I really have been enjoying it. It definitely is a what you put in is what you get out kind of tool. So that is something to realize. You won't see the value unless you are actually using it everyday or for big projects. I think the free version is awesome! The widget sometimes takes a while to update what is in my task list which is a bit of a bummer. All around though it is a pretty great tool!

The Developers for some reason have taken away the ability to organize each task as you see it listed. They say you can do it on desktop but that is not consistently. if I organize tasks according to the due date and what have been completed it populates jobs that happened over a year ago and I have to scroll through all of what was done yesterday. they're making the app more difficult to work with. subtasks don't work either

Big bait There is no option to start with the free version of the app. It starts with 30 days of the advanced version. After that time period you have to pay to continue using the functions. This means if I want to experience what I can do with the free version, I have to make an account and then wait for 30 days, which is just bs. This decision is horrible for users who just want to check out the app.

It's not syncing, no matter what I do or if I'm on a data plan or Wi-Fi. There's always a sign of how many pending changes are waiting to be synced but the syncing never happens! What would I want to havr an app that doesn't work of anyway, every time I need to make an update I have to get to the conputer

Asana has worked great, but I now have two critical issues with it. First, the android app won't connect to the internet even though I have internet connection both in mobile data and over wifi. I've tried reinstalling and force stopping, but it's been doing this for several days now. Now I can't login after reinstalling with "No network" so the app is useless. Second, the mobile app no longer allows you to reorder columns in board view, even though the web and windows app version will do this.

Ever since the update this app sucks outloud. The dashboard in the widget is useless, nothing shows up there. Things that were there before the update are gone. I have to check the calendar at best to find tasks, at worst I have to refer to the email I was sent. Making the app effectively useless. I can just use a calendar app. That way I can see the info i need at a glance without having to dig for it. UPDATE. I don't see ANY of my projects at all in the app. Just deleted the app. Thanks!!

I used to like the functionality of Asana when I last used it in a web browser some time ago. The UX on the mobile app could be improved: 1. No apparent way of renaming projects or categories. 2. The Complete Task button is too easy to click. I accidentally marked tasks as Complete while I was trying to sort the task list.

Claustrophobic App. I dont know where the options are hidden and they show so many in demos. This ought to work on mobile app alone as well instead of depending on desktop for most features to work. But simple tasks such as to add team members, or to remove old projects were not possible on mobile. Tried adding tasks but the app kept failing to connect to internet despite a high speed wifi. I had installed it earlier twice, and couldn't figure out how to work this system. Hyped app.

In theory, the app is great, but there are some limitations that make me feel like I need to keep looking for an app to help me manage my projects, tasks, etc.  The mobile app won't let you move and adjust tasks in the sticky board view, which is a major problem.  Also, when tasks are complete, I can't just check them off in the mobile app - it takes like 3 steps to check off a task.  Then, if you're using the sticky note / board view, you can't delete a column unless you first delete ALL of the tasks that are in the column - even the ones that are already complete.  This is a major hassle.

Ticket creation, deletion and editing takes at least 30 seconds to register. Creating a ticket often creates multiple copies of the ticket. "Unable to sync" error is very common. The web app works fine, just orient your phone landscape and choose 'request desktop site' in your browser menu.

The product works well but the product support is inadequate. Especially the billing support. Email only and a company policy of "credit only" in dealing with disputes. The first response to the billing inquiry was 5 business days and then an average of two days each attempt to resolve. Over two weeks for an "exception" to policy to correct a double billing where the refunded amount does not match either bill. Gave up as close enough but need a company I can count on to resolve issues quickly and correctly. Again, easy to use product but it has to have solid support as well.

I love Asana. I use it to organize lots of things such as product launches, starting a book, content creation, research, and more... I can aet tasks and give them due dates. I can collaborate my content with other freelancers. It auto saves, I can export docs, attach files, and everything is synced, my phone and computer. Awesomeness!!!!

I really like asana on the computer, but this app still has major issues. Trying to type comments on a task is extremely finicky. My cursor randomly jumps around and it force-prevents me from typing certain characters in some places. Also the text editor is way too small and non-adjustable. (I'd submit a ticket, but sounds like that doesn't work either. Maybe this will) Please! Make it amazing like the desktop version!

the mobile app experience is terrible and should not be used. I am on a tablet and nothing is done to make use of the added real estate. There is no sidebar and no easy way to see your list of projects. I ended up switching to the browser and requesting the desktop site. No thought was given to tablet usage at all -- coming from a UX designer. Asana features are great.. just let us use whatever device we want cmon.

I really like the app. I use it to keep track of daily work tasks for myself and employees. I have my monthy notes in projects and the daily breakdown under description (within projects) The one thing I find myself wishing I could do is search my note text. It seems like just projects are searchable. It would be awesome if I could search keywords in my description. So I could type in a customer name for example and it highlights anything with the name.

There are some limitations to functionally on the app that make it a bit clunky to use. The desktop version is definitely superior. For instance you can't add tasks under certain headings or add tasks to multiple projects. But it's certainly functional enough to be useful in tandem with the desktop website. I could see me eating this 5 stars with a bit more robust functionally. *It wouldn't be a resource I use SOLELY as an app.*

I've been using Asana for years and now we're looking at shifting to another platform because the mobile app has become too buggy. The app is too slow to load, cannot display fields properly, and frequently crashes or drop task changes completely. A mobile app is essential for workflow nowadays and this performance isn't up to standard.

I haven't made full use of this app yet, or actually communicate with my colleagues, I've only tested it out, and it's AMAZING!!! It has all the features you will need, keeps tasks and projects very organized, up to date, and everything of that sort. Great way to get tasks done within your organization, department, your small team, or even just yourself. I love it. And it could very much be used as a helpdesk.

Buggy mess. I receive emails about organization tasks (not mine) and the link from the email leads to the browser which then opens the app which says "Could not find what you're looking for". Next, I try to contact support from the app - it opens the website with an endless login loop "Please wait a moment while we sign you into the Asana Help Center". Two basic features, completely broken. It's like no one has even tested this app. UPD: tried emailing support@ and got "address not monitored"🤦

This app is fantastic. It's Trello for grownups. A few problems: 1. Hide completed tasks on Android. (Works on web and iPhone... come on Asana, get it together.) 2. Either move a few things, like "Predecessor" and "Start Date", to the free version, or else lower the cost of one seat for the Pro version. 5-seat minimum at $50/month is driving away otherwise loyal freelancers.

I was charged $395.64 all at once after a free trial. I thought I would be charged monthly. Wrong. The invoice I received said $0. I received an invoice stating $395.64 2 DAYS before it was deducted from my account! 2 DAYS to correct any mistake and it must be in writing to. Probably impossible to stop a bank draft in 2 DAYS! Be careful people!

Warning: do not give your credit card! I signed up last year for a free trial. It didn't do what we wanted so decided to cancel. 12 months passed and they then took £272 out of nowhere. Queried it with their support team and was told that I should have read the small print. Awful company, consider teamwork or clickup instead.

EDIT: Confirm this issue appears to have been fixed. 5 stars. Never ship an update before the Christmas holidays... this latest version has some annoying bugs. When opening and editing a task, the back buttons stop working (both the on-screen button and the Android button), so the only option is to close the app and re-open it. Also, you can only view Recent projects - under "Projects" the "All" tab is empty. Not a good experience after years of otherwise positive use of Asana.

Will change back to 5 when widgets work again. Like others have pointed out the widgets come up blank until you open the app to refresh them. Then next time you wake your phone again they are blank again. Useless app without the widget. Tried troubleshooting. Still issues. Edit 02/06/2024 : even more bugs, each update adds a slew of new bugs. Now it rarely works at all, doesn't sync anymore between participants, widget no longer will load, app crashes continuously etc.

Absolute trash. Stay away from this app and just use the web version if you manage too many tasks and uses a lot of tags and different filters. Filter doesn't work correctly. Probably, the app obverloads because there are over a thousand tasks in our project already. And one more thing, when you browse the app for more than a minute and try to reload/refresh the window it doesn't load anymore. I guess this only works for smaller project with few number of tasks. The heck.

Exceptionally efficient Android app, can also be used as desktop app, also can be used in web browser directly. Reviewing app: fantastic. Functionality is high enough to be customizable, but user friendly and intuitive enough to keep simple. Suggestion for designers: the "sort" function needs to be streamlined. I.e., it *does* work exactly as you tell it to, which is good. In my opinion though, you should be able to adjust the order of tasks/cards under a column with more ease when wanted.

Seriously, I just love this app. It is intuitive and keeps me on track. I have yet to think I wish I could do something and not be able to quickly find it. Also they design intentional joy and celebration in with unicorns and narwhals flying across the screen periodically when you finish tasks. I. LOVE. THAT. 🦄🌈✨

Terrible UI. Calendar view is very sensitive to touch and not intuitive. I scroll a little and it skips 3 months ahead. It does not remember my view preference and I have to reset it each time I open the app. Everything is too crowded and requires too much precision to click on the button I want to click on. I just want to see what my tasks are for the day, and they mess up even something simple like that

Used to love this app, but the latest update is making me tear my hair out. The default view is a useless calendar and there is no option to change it. Contacted support and only got a bot response and after asking for specific help there is no response after a week. Now takes me 10 mins to successfully get to the list view. Whatever you did Asana please undo it and bring back my favourite app of them all!!!!!

I use this app on my phone and on my computer, but this latest update took away the calendar view for me when looking at teammates assigned tasks. This was a crucial use case for me and it was just gone. Please bring back the ability to use a calendar view for other teammates, not just for myself.

Great app with a quick and easy to use interface. Only feature I'd like to request is that the app remembers the filter settings I use for my tasks. Each time I open the app I have to set the filter to order the tasks by due date and it would be great if my last setting was persisted.

Veey good user interface... Please add reminder facility... If some work is assigned to me. I should be in position to set reminder for it in app itself just like we have in Google keep.. If u add it here that to whom so ever work is assigned as per his convenience he can add reminder for doing that task... U can also add work priority.. High medium and low priority... It would be great... Overall we all liked the app...

I love and mainly use the web app, so I'm ok with the mobile app being slightly limited. But I'm impressed with the updates and how it is becoming more usable. Keep it up Asana! PS: it would be great to be able to adjust widget text size. Edit: App isn't presently loading. Will update back to five stars when it does. Edit2: cleared cache and data and logged in again, works fine now. Leaving this here in case it's useful to anyone else.

Overall great, missing 2 things: 1st, the ability to make "calendar" view the default instead of list view so it's not 2 extra clicks every time I use the app - and 2nd, when a date is selected, and you hit +new task, it should auto-populate the date with the one selected instead of blank... 5+ years ago the app used to do that, but they changed it.

Was great. But lately when you type a lot of text in, the app begins to blur the text on the screen. Especially when you start scrolling up or down. Looks like it can no longer handle large amounts of text. Hope Asana can fix this soon. This never used to happen!

Asana ended up costing me $250 in fines and a pile of hassle. Want one inbox? Good luck? At some point, they changed My Tasks to have different tabs for Recently Assigned and for Later. Well, we missed a recurring tax from the city, and now we're out $250. The whole reason I use project management is to not miss stuff. That, and I can't see overdues by person in the mobile app. Kind of important, don't you think? I hate migrating software, but 5 years of this is enough. Time for Basecamp.

Like the web service okay. Mobile app is unusable. It only show the first 60 characters of a task. Sounds like enough, but my job assigns these repeat tasks with the project identifyer at the end... making the mobile app useless for me. Also i would not have given a bad review if there had been a way to give feedback within the app. I just wanted the developers to know the product is not meeting my needs and i suspect others as well.

App version often crashes like several times an hour. Some edited Settings reverts back on default even without my intervention. Listing is not too organized. I'm trying this for personal use but it just does not deliver. How much more so if i integrate this on my business? Where hundreds of tasks befall per project? This definitely need some improvement. This is my first time using a project management tool and unfortunately, I wasn't satisfied at all. Dunno if trello is any better tho.

Long time on and off user. Have loved the innovation and ideas over time. Sad that 'pay what you can afford' models aren't more accessible for those of us without disposable incomes. Three Qualms: 1. You have no easily accessible feedback area; 2. My Android app doesn't have the sort alphabetically; 3. My sort and filter preferences don't remain static when I return to the app... Very annoying.

I honestly love this app but I've been a customer for an entire year paying for 5 seats of a premium plan. Initially I was told a plan with less than 5 seats wasn't possible. Then they're offered that very same thing to a "random" selection of customers. Yet, I'm still paying for 3 extra seats I don't need. I want to also upgrade to a business plan, but I can't because I'd have to pay an additional $91 for unused seats. You are roadblocking punishing loyalty. Do better.

First up. Asana is great, haven't paid a $. I've been through a bunch of programmes, Asana is the only one that hasn't been rendered close to unusable by flaws (sometimes rectifiable with $upgrade). Asana upgrades seem worth the dollars v productivity if you need them. I highly recommend it and do. This app. As a companion to the main programme on the go it could be great. The filter options are limited and reset every time you leave the programme. Horrible flaw in such powerful software
Open app to update today's time, but app gives an error when tryying to add time against a task.

Downloaded to try for a team project. Can't locate time tracking functions for workload. And made a mistake in spelling my last name. Profile edit doesn't work & allows me to edit everything except my name. I have uninstalled now because simple things should not be this difficult.

I have been using this app for nearly 10years now and most of the improvements /updates are great. However, of late, I've realised that any task created defaults to 'private' which is a good thing I guess, except that, you can only change it to 'public ' by logging onto a pc which is not always practical :( - FAO: Asana inc, a fix to that would be perfect. Nonetheless, a very good app overall :)

Love love love Asana! Both the desktop and mobile version are great. Super easy to use, very intuitive and includes a wide range of features (even just with the free version). Would highly recommend, I'm highly addicted to using it both at work and for myself.

Almost exactly what I was looking for. Tremendously flexible but with a lot of power behind it. Being able to visualize things in multiple different ways is an absolute game changer: lists are handy in some cases, but for my weekly planning being and to see a calendar view is perfect, and having a board is what I need when I'm not the only one doing stuff. Oh - and automating activity (like notifying me when someone changes a status)? Absolutely epic.

No backgrounds! When ever are you going to allow users to choose backgrounds? Trello, Wunderlist, even Microsoft Tasks has awesome backgrounds and make using the app visually absolutely a pleasure .. but not Asana, Asana chooses to remain boring and unpleasant looking! Seriously, how hard would it be to make your app better looking, online web windows and iOS etc?

A rip off. I unsubscribed, when I found the platform clunky and cluttered. Despite this, I was still billed, and haven't received a refund. The cancellation process is adversarial, being hidden and requiring many click troughs to confirm again and again.

The mobile app feels a bit like an afterthought... lots of features from desktop are missing. The widget could be the most useful thing except you cannot change the views and the default of "recently assigned" isn't very helpful. Should default to today's tasks or offer calendar view at least.

A word of warning. Once you sign up for an account, they will SPAM you to death. I receive at least 5 emails and texts daily. I only signed up a couple weeks ago and went to sign in to delete the account, but it says my email address is unknown – but I still receive the emails. Rules and filters have been applied. It might be a useful app, but I think there are adequate tools in google calendar and you have control over that information. They also do not send you SPAM.

At first looked very promising (specially UI) but dude, it lacks the very basic feature: the due date alerts. Really!!!? I tried to work my way around to sync my tasks with Google calendar to get alerts from there. But after the initial sync it takes 24 hrs or may be more to sync again. So may be you will miss out on tasks that u add with dhe date in next 24 hrs. I found Project Buddy but it is paid service. It is useless for me now, I have to go back to Trello bcz it has due date notifications.

It does not work in mobile phone ... this application is very expensive and you can not see the same dashboard in the mobile device that you are seeing in your computer. No usefull at all. I have 2 dashboards to follow my tasks and you can only see a list in the mobile device... which does not make sense because my tasks are in 2 dashboards that are not available in the mobile device. Still a lot to develop in the mobile application because you do not get the information as in your computer.

Even after setting up a email and password, it tells me that my credentials are invalid right away. I thought it was an issue with my LastPass account at first but no, it definitely recorded my password correctly for the auto fill function to work. This app is glitching out on me for doing exactly what I'm supposed to do to start an account. Needs to be fixed asap.

I'm not able to login with my company email, it kept askin me to input my google account and deleting device policy, once it's done, it's just showing "authenticating with Google" but it never log in. You got to fix this asap. How can we trust your apps, when you are not even able to fix the login/registration process from your apps.

Update: I was contacted and the issue has been resolved. I installed it to test it out. It's not quite right for me. Now it's not allowing me to delete my account and spamming me with notification emails daily! Asana admin - please help and I will amend my review once the issue has been corrected.

Really good app for having if you work on a business or even for your day to day tasks. Perfect for having a list to what to do. One thing I would change to or better said I would add for free accounts is to add hours as well not just days and if the deadline is closer to end send notifications on the phone not just emails

Like the app. Will love it when the add the ability to mark tasks complete/reschedule tasks from the notification bar. Right now you can comment on tasks from the notification bar, but those other functionalities are what will really make Asana earn the #1 task manager slot (at least for me). [Android O, Motorola Droid Maxx 2]

Update: was a two start review but I'm the last few years Asana has vastly improved the app where I don't see any bugs with it. Really love this app as it helps me keep on track and keep my team members productive as well. Old review: Love this software all around. Mobile app is good too, except one tragic flaw... often tasks don't get submitted because the internet isn't available. Clears them out instead.

I so wish this app rocked and we could use it like the desktop. Much improved from how slow it used to be, but we still can't use several features that we use regularly on desktop (copy a task) and the website forces use of the app from a mobile device so there isn't a workaround.

Not impressed. The app makes it hard to find projects, email links lead to a general page and not the project you wanted. Should have smelled a rat when they block access via browser on a phone and force the app. Just let me access it on Firefox and upload pictures from my phone.

Awesome app for organizing your work. I use it to jot down to do things under the appropriate categories and that already makes them onto actionable items. Love how it helps me break down mountains of work into bite size chunks. And being free makes it a winner !!!

Falls far short to its online big brother. I use this every day and the web version is fantastic. The app feels incomplete and doesn't give the same feel. I find not all of my tasks appear after syncing. We are all still waiting on the offline desktop version as well.
usually would be a 4 star but not working since yesterday's update, help?

I use Asana on my laptop all the time and it works great. Tried to use on my Pixel 6 and as soon as I log in it crashes. If I try to reopen it, it immediately crashes. I tried restarting my phone, no luck. I uninstalled and reinstalled Asana - initially it loaded correctly but as soon as I logged in (using Google account) once again it crashed and repeatedly crashes each time. What a pain, hope they fix this asap.

This app never gives the notification at right time. Always delays. Whenever I open the app, the notification pops up. It makes it more frustrating. O'wise UI was terrible anyways. My client has hundreds of tasks, if she comments on one, I can never figure out which one it is. Show some blowing dot on the task itself so that it stands out!! Dumbass!!

App works great, but you need to get the paid version if you want to use integrations, and it's pretty expensive. Doesn't seem worth the price, but the free version is great if you don't mind a few dozen extra clicks everyday to line up your platforms.

This app really is quite useful but there's a couple of key aspects that need improving. We can no longer reorder tasks as we see them listings could we have been able to in the past but no more. In the past we could organize tasks according to what had been completed and it would take us to the tasks immediately at the top of the page that has been completed yesterday. now we have to scroll through a year-and-a-half of events. also sub tasks to a main task will not pop up on a given date.

Thank you so much for fixing this! My company LIVES on Asana! I was hoping this last update would fix an issue noted below in other reviews. All of a sudden, trying to type in the description on the mobile app, it double and triple spaces everything, moves your cursor and starts randomly typing in the middle of the description. Never had an issue like this before - please fix!

As a small business owner with 4 kids and a dose of ADD this app is a life saver for me! I can set up repeating tasks/reminders, checking done boxes is a delight, and it is super easy to move a task from one place to another. Asana makes it much easier for me to track my client jobs and stay on task.

It's a good app, but it doesn't have all the same views as the online desktop version, such as the calendar and timeline and that is a bummer. Otherwise works fine. Every now and then has syncing issues where it doesn't update immediately.

I've tried several organization tools like Asana (zenkit, infinity, Trello, airtable) but none for me as well as Asana does. I can organize and color code tasks from my jobs or personal life individually and view them all in the calendar view. Integrations with outlook, Gmail and Google calendar make this a 5 start app. I'm glad there's a free tired that offers all these great features. I wouldn't mind paying a monthly fee if it was closer to $5 than $10.

Keeps me organized! I use this for my college assignments, work projects, and assignments as a Graduate Assistant. The only thing that I wish could be fixed is that the tasks used to be color coded to their project and now they all have the same color. Please being the color coding back!

Wasn't what I was looking for but can't deleta account because when I signed up for the free account I was automatically upgraded to a "paid" account and am not PERMITTED to delete my account. I am forced to come back after to delete my account. I created tge account to try out tge badic features, to see if it met my needs, nit to be forced into a relationship with this company. It wasn't something I opted into, there was no asking, it was forced and unwanted. It is unacceptable and a turn off.

I like Asana. I really do. But I've been using the Kanban Board thru app recently with my colleagues and it is such disappointing to see that we cannot filter/ sort view on it. Please add that function as we do operate on our mobile phone most of the time
The app logged me out and the webpage it opens when I click "log in" wont load.

once again, whatever you did with the latest update screwed something up. Now it is impossible to save the view so it is sorted by due date. The sort options from the main app just don't save, and they are not reflected in the android widget either.

It's a great tool. Not fulfilling all of my needs as some essential features are missing, thus I needed to switch to something else. But, I was using it for years, it's stable, fast, solid UI, and if you don't mind little more complexity - much better than Trello IMO.

i dont see notifications, feel problems in setting reminder time however it should be flexible so that we can set time in minutes and notification sound cannot be changed or set, we should be able to set any sound we want from our phone for notifications the way we want... push notifications should go like a call or sms buzzing the one to wake up and do the task. so should be for self reminder. i look forward to give 5 stars soon when all is fixed by u expert people 😊

Asana is a great tool and this app is really nice too but there are 2 things I believe the team needs to focus on. 1. Notifications come in late or not at all. Most times I have to manually open the app before all notifications start pouring in. We use this tool for real-time collaboration and that is a major pain. 2. Can we have a feature built in that allows us switch organisations? Some users work in multiple organisations that use Asana and will like to easily switch the way Slack allows.

Update: works properly after following techadvice. Usually a great app, but I have found in recent days that when I scroll through tasks it goes back to the top automatically, and when I open pictures it shows the wrong images. Using the app is annoying especially for a paid software. Using mi9 most recent updates.

It's okay, lacks any intelligence with changes. For example I move a due date on one task, and the dependent task schedule does not automatically adjust. And the cost model to get what should be basic or mid level pricing is not appropriate. I hate SAAS, it is a pure money grab.

I love Asana! I use it for my personal task management, in addition to using it at work. It's been life changing and I'm so much more organized. I've also hooked it up to my Amazon Alexa so I can add tasks by voice using IFTTT. Recent improvements to the app made a HUGE different and dramatically improved the UI -- thank you!

Love Asana, hate their Android app UPDATE 11/25/24 The Android app continues to be pure garbage. There have been ongoing major synchronization issues with the Android app where changes don't appear and the app keeps locking up and freezing. Updating tasks, setting task schedules, sorting and organizing tasks, and really any changes whatsoever that you make on the Android app over 50% of the time don't appear properly and you have to reload the app to see changes and clear cache to fix crashes.

1. only around 15 tasks are displayed in "My Tasks" tab instead of the full list. 2. when rearranging the tasks' order it returns the task to the section initially moved from. 3. when swiping down to refresh the page the sync symbol freezes. Previously I encountered other issues which ended up being fixed, however the constant new problems with this app make me keep going back to my laptop - please be more cautious when releasing app updates, or enable web version on mobile instead of app only

Reinstalled app after not using for several years. Was unable to clear any old projects via delete or archive. In attempting to create a new project, I could not add details as the screen displayed on my phone did not seem to match the attempted rendering size of the app (3 dot menu partially off screen and onscreen keyboard would not pop up). I'm not wasting anymore time on an app that is freshly installed and not working. Hope you can fix it for others though.

at this point you can't scroll through your tasks through the day without jumping back to 2024. if you try to move to the next day, the calendar goes blank and goes to the same month in the previous year. this app has been terrible for the last 2 months. not sure what has happened but I'm ready to throw my phone through window. my staff and I use this app all day everyday and everyone is just as frustrated as I am

I'd debated on either using Asna or Click Up, and decided to use Asana, which was a big mistake. Although I used it for years and loved it, I tried changing my email and ended up running into MASSIVE errors. It duplicated my account, and then no longer allowed me access through mobile. But when I tried to delete the duplicate account it completely removed me from all of my accounts. Customer service couldn't assist me and I've lost everything. Which was hundreds of hours of work and organization

I love how user-friendly it is and that I can use it on my laptop and my phone. I really like to be organized but also need easy ways to do it that are visually appealing and Asana meets those needs for me. I am also starting to use it with my team members and it's great - keeps everything organized!
